LEGAL.HOME_TAGLINE: Web-based administrative management software for Christian churches and congregations.
LEGAL.HOME_IDENTITY_P1: The official name of this application is AIC — Administração de Igrejas Cristãs. This is the same name shown on Google’s OAuth consent screen and throughout the product brand. AIC is not a Google product and is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by Google.
LEGAL.HOME_IDENTITY_P2: The platform is operated by the church or entity that hosts the instance. The software lets congregation administrative teams manage internal church data and processes.
LEGAL.HOME_PURPOSE_P1: AIC — Administração de Igrejas Cristãs exists to help Christian churches run day-to-day congregation administration in one system: people, secretariat, ministries, teaching, finance, and communication.
LEGAL.HOME_PURPOSE_P2: Instead of scattered spreadsheets and tools, the church records members and families, organizes the worship and event calendar, tracks visitors and attendance, issues and archives documents, builds volunteer schedules, follows small groups and classes, records financial entries, and publishes internal announcements.
LEGAL.HOME_PURPOSE_P3: Only users authorized by the church (for example pastors, secretaries, leaders, and administrators) can sign in. The public can read this homepage, the Privacy Policy, and the Terms of Service without logging in.
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURES_INTRO: Below is a full description of the main capabilities available to authorized users:
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_1: Members and families: records, status, transfers between congregations, and birthday reports.
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_2: Secretariat calendar: create and edit congregation events (services, meetings, rehearsals, weddings, and other types), with simple recurrence and ICS file export/import.
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_3: Visitors, attendance, and official secretariat documents.
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_4: Ministries and volunteer schedules linked to calendar events.
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_5: Small groups and Sunday Bible school (classes, enrollments, and attendance).
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_6: Finance and assets: entries, categories, reports, and church property.
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_7: Internal announcements, notifications, user management, roles, and permissions.
LEGAL.HOME_FEATURE_8: Optional integration that syncs the secretariat calendar with a calendar on a Google account authorized by the congregation.
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_TITLE: Why we request Google user data
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_INTRO: AIC — Administração de Igrejas Cristãs integrates with Google Calendar only as an optional secretariat calendar feature. We request Google user data only when an authorized user starts the OAuth connection. In line with the Google API Services User Data Policy, we explain who is requesting data, what data, and why.
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_WHO_BODY: The application AIC — Administração de Igrejas Cristãs requests the data on behalf of the congregation that operates the instance. The connection is started by a user with secretariat write permission and is tied to the active congregation (it is not a mandatory personal calendar for every member).
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_WHAT_BODY: We request only the scopes needed to sync events for the selected calendar:
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_WHAT_1: Event metadata and content from the selected calendar (for example title, description, location, times, recurrence, and event identifiers).
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_WHAT_2: The list of calendars on the authorized account, so the user can choose which calendar to sync.
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_WHAT_3: OAuth tokens (access and refresh), stored encrypted on the AIC server, so sync can continue without asking for the Google password again.
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_WHY_TITLE: Why do we request this data?
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_WHY_BODY: We use this data solely to keep the congregation calendar in AIC aligned with the authorized Google calendar: create, update, or delete events according to the configured sync direction (two-way, AIC → Google calendar only, or Google calendar → AIC only). The user benefit is avoiding duplicate data entry and keeping church services, meetings, and events consistent in both systems.
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_LIMITED_TITLE: Limited use and what we do not do
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_LIMITED_BODY: We do not sell Google data. We do not use Google data for advertising, remarketing, credit decisions, or ad profiling. We do not transfer Google Calendar data to data brokers or to third parties for purposes unrelated to calendar sync, except when required by law. Sync can be disconnected at any time in the secretariat calendar or in Google Account permissions.
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_PRIVACY_LINK_PREFIX: Full details about access, storage, sharing, and deletion are in our
LEGAL.HOME_GOOGLE_PRIVACY_LINK_SUFFIX: (the same link provided on the OAuth consent screen).
LEGAL.HOME_ACCESS_BODY: This homepage, the Privacy Policy, and the Terms of Service are public. The rest of the system requires sign-in with a church-authorized account.
LEGAL.HOME_FOOTER_BRANDING: AIC — Administração de Igrejas Cristãs integrates with Google Calendar. Google and Google Calendar are trademarks of Google LLC. This application is not affiliated with or endorsed by Google.

PRIVACY.INTRO: This Privacy Policy explains how the AIC application (Christian Church Administration) collects, uses, and protects information when you use the platform, including when you connect a Google account to sync the congregation calendar.
PRIVACY.S1_BODY: Congregation and user data in AIC are processed by the organization that operates the application instance (the church or administering entity). For Google integrations, AIC acts as a technical tool that processes data under that organization’s instructions.
PRIVACY.S2_ITEM_1: AIC account data: name, email, credentials, and access permissions.
PRIVACY.S2_ITEM_2: Church operational data: members, events, documents, and financial records, as used in the platform.
PRIVACY.S2_ITEM_3: Google Calendar data (only if sync is authorized): events from the selected calendar (title, description, location, times, recurrence) and technical identifiers needed for the AIC ↔ Google link.
PRIVACY.S2_ITEM_4: Google OAuth tokens (access and refresh), stored encrypted on the AIC server to maintain sync.
PRIVACY.S3_TITLE: 3. How Google Calendar data is used
PRIVACY.S3_BODY: AIC — Administração de Igrejas Cristãs uses Google Calendar access only to provide the secretariat calendar sync feature that is visible in the congregation calendar interface:
PRIVACY.S3_ITEM_1: Create, update, or delete events on the authorized Google calendar and/or in the AIC calendar, according to the chosen sync direction.
PRIVACY.S3_ITEM_2: Maintain the technical link between AIC events and Google events (identifiers and tokens) to avoid duplicates.
PRIVACY.S3_ITEM_3: Show connection status and allow users to disconnect sync.
PRIVACY.S3B_BODY: Use of data obtained via Google Calendar scopes is limited to providing or improving that user-facing sync feature. We do not transfer or sell that data to advertising platforms, data brokers, or information resellers. We do not use that data for ads, remarketing, credit, or lending. We do not allow humans to read that data except with the user’s affirmative agreement for specific data, for security/abuse investigation, to comply with law, or when data is aggregated for internal operations under applicable law.
PRIVACY.S4_BODY: OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest. Communication with the Google API uses HTTPS. Connecting or disconnecting Google Calendar is limited to users with secretariat write permission on the active congregation.
PRIVACY.S5_BODY: The Google connection is per congregation. Synced events exist both in AIC and in the linked Google calendar. After disconnecting, AIC stops syncing; events already copied may remain in both systems until removed manually. Tokens are invalidated or removed on disconnect to the extent technically possible.
PRIVACY.S6_BODY: You can disconnect Google Calendar in the secretariat Agenda (AIC), revoke access at https://myaccount.google.com/permissions, and ask your church administration to delete or correct personal data processed in AIC, as applicable under local law (including LGPD where relevant).
PRIVACY.S7_BODY: For privacy questions about this AIC instance, contact the church or entity that operates the platform. For product questions, use the support channel provided by the instance operator.

TERMS.INTRO: These Terms of Service govern use of the AIC application (Christian Church Administration), including the optional Google Calendar integration.
TERMS.S1_BODY: By accessing or using AIC, you agree to these Terms and the Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the platform or connect Google accounts.
TERMS.S2_BODY: AIC is a management platform for churches (members, secretariat, finance, and related features). Google Calendar sync is an optional feature that keeps the congregation agenda aligned with a Google calendar authorized via OAuth.
TERMS.S3_BODY: You are responsible for keeping your AIC credentials confidential and for actions taken under your account. Only users authorized by the congregation should connect Google Calendar. The congregation is responsible for the content of synced events and for complying with applicable laws and internal policies.
TERMS.S4_BODY: Use of the Google Calendar API is also subject to Google’s Terms of Service and the Google API Services User Data Policy. AIC requests only the scopes needed for event sync. You may revoke access at any time in AIC or in your Google account.
TERMS.S5_BODY: You must not use AIC unlawfully, abusively, or in ways that compromise system security, other users, or third-party data. Do not bypass permission controls or exploit the Google integration beyond congregation calendar sync.
TERMS.S6_BODY: AIC is provided “as is” to the extent permitted by law. We do not guarantee uninterrupted availability or error-free sync with third-party services (including Google). Temporary network or Google API failures do not void the rest of these Terms.
TERMS.S7_BODY: We may update these Terms periodically. The update date will appear on this page. Continued use of AIC after material changes constitutes acceptance of the updated version, unless applicable law provides otherwise.
